headline: Washington seeks to stay perfect against reeling Oregon squad
subhead: The Huskies' resilience tested by a Ducks team desperate to avoid an 0-3 hole.

xFactor.why: Oregon's defense must create early pressure and force Washington into mistakes; the Huskies have shown they can be rattled in tight spots, as evidenced by the Miami scare.

keyMatchup: Washington's offensive line blocking downfield versus Oregon's defensive edge rush in space—if the Huskies can establish clean pocket time, their rhythm offense should cruise; if Oregon gets penetration, it could disrupt timing and create negative plays.
body: Washington enters Eugene undefeated after a sloppy but ultimately convincing win over Miami, a performance that revealed both the Huskies' firepower and their susceptibility to defensive pressure. The ranking reflects genuine quality, but last week's fourth-quarter wobble suggests they are not invincible. Oregon, meanwhile, has now dropped consecutive close contests and faces an existential moment—losses to this Husky team would leave them buried in conference play with little margin for recovery.

The Ducks' near-miss against LSU proved they can play competitively with ranked opponents, but proximity to victories without actually winning them compounds pressure and erodes locker room confidence. Washington's track record of finishing games, however narrow the margins, gives them a clear edge in a Week 3 battle where momentum and belief become as important as talent. Look for the Huskies to lean on their balanced attack to control possession and clock, starving Oregon of possessions.
